checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-012 state observable repository artifacts, status relationships, provenance fields, lifecycle boundaries, runtime outcomes, integration points, regression surfaces, synchronized versions, and bypass conditions; AC-001 through AC-014 provide concrete fixture, inspection, and exit-code validation surfaces.

Repository/CI/quality-gate enforcement is covered by AC-003 through AC-005 and AC-009 through AC-010; behavior preservation by AC-010, AC-012, and AC-013; bounded historical treatment by AC-006 and AC-011; and deterministic POSIX/PowerShell parity by AC-002, AC-008, and AC-014.

Every acceptance criterion defines an inspectable outcome: runtime exit status and rule IDs, accepted or rejected fixtures, explicit invocation paths, traceable records, passing suites, synchronized version surfaces, or fail-closed diagnostics.

The Non-goals explicitly exclude fabricated historical reviews, policy changes, replacement of stage prechecks, installer/uninstaller behavior changes, and network or non-standard runtime dependencies; constraints additionally bound legacy selection and Done transitions.

Material migration, compatibility, approval, architecture, contract, release synchronization, runtime parity, provenance, and historical-safety constraints are explicit in REQ-003 through REQ-012, Non-goals, Constraints, and the investigation safety constraints.

The identified high-risk surfaces have planned validation: predecessor and lifecycle bypasses in AC-003 and AC-005, forged or stale provenance in AC-004, overbroad legacy exceptions in AC-006, runtime/line-ending parity in AC-008, enforcement integration in AC-009 and AC-010, historical provenance in AC-011, regression preservation in AC-012, and fail-closed behavior in AC-014.
